title: Prakas #004.11 on the Implementation of Listing Rules_110503
title_en: Prakas #004.11 on the Implementation of Listing Rules_110503
short_name: Prakas #004.11 on the Implementation of Listing Rules_110503

provisions.content:
To set forth the implementation of the Listing Rules of the Cambodia Securities
Exchange (CSX) as enclosed in the annex of this Prakas.

Director General of the Securities and Exchange Commission of Cambodia,
the Department of Securities Market Supervision, the Department of Legal Affairs, all other
departments and unit of the SECC, the Cambodia Securities Exchange, members and
participants of the Cambodia Securities Exchange, and relevant persons shall effectively
implement this Prakas from this signing date onwards.

Phnom Penh, 03 May 2011

(Signature and Seal)
Recipients: KEAT CHHON
- General Secretariat of Senate
- General Secretariat of National Assembly
- General Secretariat of Royal Government
- Cabinet of Samdech Akka Moha Sena Padei Techo Hun Sen
Prime Minister of the Kingdom of Cambodia
- Cabinets of Excellencies Deputy Prime Ministers
- Office of the Council of Ministers
- All ministries and relevant institutions
“to be informed”
- As prescribed in Article 2
“to implement”
Unofficial Translation

- Royal Gazette
- Archives-records

2

Annex of the Prakas No 004/11 SECC PrK
dated on May 03, 2011

Listing Rules
of the Cambodia Securities Exchange

provisions.chapter: Chapter I: General Provision
provisions.title: -Purpose
provisions.content:
The purpose of these rules is to prescribe the necessary matters for listing of equity
securities at the Cambodia Securities Exchange (written hereafter as “the CSX”) pursuant to
the Law on the Issuance and Trading of Non-Government Securities and relevant regulations.

provisions.chapter: Chapter I: General Provision
provisions.title: -Definitions
provisions.content:
The terms used in these rules are defined in this article and in the glossary of the annex
of the Law on Issuance and Trading of Non-Government Securities and the Anukret on the
Implementation of the Law on the Issuance and Trading of Non-Government Securities and
relevant regulations.
-“Applicant for Initial Listing” shall refer to a person who intends to apply for listing
equity securities (shares) and is not yet listed at the CSX.
-“Listed Entity” shall refer to a person who has made a public offer and been listed at
the CSX.
-“Largest Shareholder” shall refer to shareholders and the specially-related persons of
a listed company who hold the largest number of voting shares of the company. In this case,
the shares held for the purpose of portfolio investment by the institutional investors, shall be
excluded from the calculation of the number of shares held by the largest shareholder.

provisions.chapter: Chapter II: Listing of Equity Securities
provisions.title: General Provisions
provisions.content: Unofficial Translation

provisions.chapter: Chapter II: Listing of Equity Securities
provisions.title: -Preparation of Financial Statements
provisions.content:
The financial statements which shall be attached with the application for listing shall be
prepared and audited pusuant to the Law on Corporate Accounts, Their Audit and Acounting
Professions and relevant laws and regulations of the Kingdom of Cambodia.
The detailed financial information required in these rules shall be prepared based on
the financial statement stated in paragraph 1 of this article.
3

provisions.chapter: Chapter II: Listing of Equity Securities
provisions.title: -Unreturnable Applications for Listing Eligibility Review and
provisions.content:
Listing
All relevant applications for listing eligibility review and listing required by these rules
shall not be returned, although this application have been rejected or withdrawn.

provisions.chapter: Chapter II: Listing of Equity Securities
provisions.title: Requirements and Procedures for Listing Eligibility Review
provisions.content: Requirements and Procedures for Listing Eligibility Review

provisions.chapter: Chapter II: Listing of Equity Securities
provisions.title: -Listing Eligibility Review
provisions.content:
A person who intends to be listed at the CSX shall undergo the listing eligibility
review.

provisions.chapter: Chapter II: Listing of Equity Securities
provisions.title: -Application for Listing Eligibility Review

provisions.chapter: Chapter II: Listing of Equity Securities
provisions.title: -Notification of Listing Eligibility Review Result
provisions.content:
The CSX shall notify the result of the listing eligibility review to the applicant no
longer than 01 (one) month from the date of receiving the application.
In case that the CSX rejects the applicant’s listing eligibility, the CSX shall provide
reasons for the rejections in writing and the applicant may request for clarification from
the CSX and/or appeal to the SECC for review and decision.

provisions.chapter: Chapter II: Listing of Equity Securities
provisions.title: Unofficial Translation
provisions.content: Listing Requirements and Procedures

provisions.chapter: Chapter II: Listing of Equity Securities
provisions.title: -Listing Procedures
provisions.content:
A Person received the notification of elibility for listing at the CSX shall fulfill
procedures for official listing at the CSX within seven (7) working days after the securities
have been alloted to the subscribers.
4

provisions.chapter: Chapter II: Listing of Equity Securities
provisions.title: -Documents Submitted to the CSX

provisions.chapter: Chapter II: Listing of Equity Securities
provisions.title: -Official Listing
provisions.content:
After correctly and fully receiving documents as stated in the article 9 of these rules,
the CSX shall review and officially list the eligible applicant at the CSX within five (5)
working days.

provisions.chapter: Chapter II: Listing of Equity Securities
provisions.title: -Securities Listing Agreement
provisions.content:
Before permitting to be officially listed at the CSX, the eligible applicant shall sign a
securities listing agreement in accordance with the form approved by the SECC.
The listing agreement stated in paragraph 1 shall be valid from the listing date.

provisions.chapter: Chapter II: Listing of Equity Securities
provisions.title: -Quantitative Requirements
provisions.content:
The eligible applicant can officially be listed at the CSX by satisfying the following
requirements unless dertermined otherwise by the Director General of the SECC:
1- Number of shareholders holding less than 1% (one percent) voting shares:
number of shareholders holding less than 1% voting shares, who hold 10
(ten) shares or more, shall be at least 200 (two hundred) as of the date of
fulfilling the official listing procedures.
2- Number of shares held by shareholders holding less than 1% (one percent)
voting shares: voting shares of shareholders holding less than 1% (one
percent) voting shares shall be more than 200,000 (two hundred thousand)
or 15% of the total voting shares, whichever is larger;

provisions.chapter: Chapter II: Listing of Equity Securities
provisions.title: -Non-Quantitative Requirements
provisions.content:
The eligible applicant can officially be listed at the CSX by fulfiling the following non-
quantitative requirements:
Unofficial Translation

1- Share ownership of the largest shareholders shall not be changed for the last
01 (one) year until the official listing;
2- All the issued securities shall be deposited at the Operator of Securities
Depository approved by the SECC.

5

provisions.chapter: Chapter III: Conditions and Procedures of Official Delisting
provisions.title: -Official Delisting Criteria
provisions.content:
The CSX shall officially delist securities of a listed company, which fall under any of
the following cases, unless otherwise determined by the Director General of the SECC:
1- Where a listed company fails to submit the annual report for the latest fiscal
year within three (3) months after the submission deadline determined by
the SECC;
2- Where the auditor’s opinion in the audit report of a listed company is
adverse or disclaimed for 02 (two) years;
3- Where the annual reports show that the shareholders’ equity for the last 02
(two) consecutive years has decreased more than 50% (fifty percent).
4- Where there is repetition of breaches of disclosure obligations;
5- Where a listed company is not able to succesfully settle the commercial
paper, or issued cheque, or the transactions with commercial banks have
been suspended;
6- Where a listed company falls under any causes of corporate dissolution
pursuant to the relevant laws and regulations;
7- Where a listed company is restricted to transfer its shares. Nevertheless, this
provision shall not be applied to the cases where the restriction on shares
transfer is not deemed to impede the trading in the Market, as the restriction
is imposed by other laws unrelated to the securities sector.

provisions.chapter: Chapter III: Conditions and Procedures of Official Delisting
provisions.title: -Public Notice and Trading Permission of Official Delisted
provisions.content:
Securities.
When delisting any listed securities, the CSX shall make public notice on the delisting
of the concerned securities.
Where there is any delisting of any securities, the CSX may permit the continuation of
trading the concerned securities for 10 (ten) days, unless otherwise determined by the Director
General of the SECC.

provisions.chapter: Chapter IV: Supervision of Listed Securities
provisions.title: -Obligation and Procedures of Disclosing Information
provisions.content:
Unofficial Translation

Listed entity shall timely submit to the CSX the disclosure documents and other related
documents to fulfill their obligation of disclosure determined by the SECC.

provisions.chapter: Chapter IV: Supervision of Listed Securities
provisions.title: -Lock-up Agreement
provisions.content:
Applicant for initial listing shall attach the agreement in which states that shareholders
with controlling interest in voting share shall not sell or transfer their shares for a period of at

6

least one year, and shareholders owning at least 15% (fifteen percent) of total shares shall not
sell or transfer their shares for a period of at least six months after the initial listing.

provisions.chapter: Chapter IV: Supervision of Listed Securities
provisions.title: -Trading Suspension and Trade Resumption

provisions.chapter: Chapter IV: Supervision of Listed Securities
provisions.title: -Application for Additional Share Issuance
provisions.content:
A listed entity, who intends to issue additional shares, shall complete procedures for
listing stated in these rules.

provisions.chapter: Chapter IV: Supervision of Listed Securities
provisions.title: -Application for Listing Change
provisions.content:
In case of changes in the name of the company, par value of the listed securities or
the number of shares issued, the listed entity shall complete procedures for listing changes at
the CSX.

provisions.chapter: Chapter IV: Supervision of Listed Securities
provisions.title: -Notification of Securities Forgery
provisions.content:
Where there is any notice in writing to the SECC of any forgery in transfer of
securities, a listed entity shall immediately notify the CSX. The notification may be done
through the Securities Transfer Agent.

provisions.chapter: Chapter V: Listing fee
provisions.title: -Listing Eligibility Review Examination and Listing Fees
provisions.content:
A person applied for listing eligibility review shall pay the examination fee of
4,000,000 (four million) Riels to the CSX.
A listed entity shall pay the listing fee to the CSX of 0.10% (zero point ten percent) of
the total of the market capitalization based on the price of the initial public offering.
Unofficial Translation

provisions.chapter: Chapter V: Listing fee
provisions.title: -Annual Listing Fee
provisions.content:
A listed entity shall pay annual fees to the CSX based on the market capitalization at
the end of each fiscal year as the follows:
1- If the market capitalization of less than or equal to 12, 000,000,000 (twelve
billion) Riels, the fee shall be 3,000,000 (three million) Riels.

7

2- If the market capitalization of more than 12,000,000,000 ( twelve billion) Riels,
but less than or equal to 40,000,000,000 (forty billion) Riels, the fees shall be
paid as the follows:
a. Market capitalization of 12,000,000,000 (twelve billion) Riels:
3,000,000 (three million) Riels; and
b. For the remaining of the market capitalization, fee shall be 0.02 %.
3- If the market capitalization of more than 40,000,000,0000 (forty billion) Riels,
but equal to or less than 200,000,000,000 (two hundred billion) Riels, the fees
shall be paid as the follows:
a. Market capitalization of 12,000,000,000 (twelve billion) Riels:
3,000,000 (three million) Riels;
b. Market capitalization of 28,000,000,000 (twenty eight billion) Riels:
5,600,000 (five million and six hundred thousand) Riels; and
c. For the remaining of the market capitalization, fee shall be 0.015%.
4- If the market capitalization of more than 200,000,000,000 (two hundred billion)
Riels, the fees shall be paid as the follows:
a. Market capitalization of 12,000,000,000 (twelve billion) Riels:
3,000,000 Riels;
b. Market capitalization of 28,000,000,000 (twenty eight billion) Riels:
5,600,000 (five million and six hundred thousand);
c. Market capitalization of 160,000,000,000 (one hundred sixty
billion), fee shall be 0.015% or equal to 24,000,000 (twenty four
million) Riels; and
d. For the remaining market capitalization, fee shall be 0.005%.

provisions.chapter: Chapter VI: Transitional Provision
provisions.title: -Excemption
provisions.content:
In the initial two year-period in implementing these rules, on an actual basis, the CSX
may request to the SECC for waiving any requirements of listing eligibility review and official
listing for an applicant for initial listing.

********
Unofficial Translation

8
